name: wp-block-development
description: Use when developing WordPress (Gutenberg) blocks: block.json metadata, register_block_type(_from_metadata), attributes/serialization, supports, dynamic rendering (render.php/render_callback), deprecations/migrations, viewScript vs viewScriptModule, and @wordpress/scripts/@wordpress/create-block build and test workflows.
capabilities: Configure block.json metadata including apiVersion 3, scripts, styles, and render
Register blocks with register_block_type_from_metadata in PHP
Implement dynamic rendering with render.php and get_block_wrapper_attributes
Add deprecations and migrations for backward compatibility
Debug common block issues like invalid blocks and attribute persistence
author: Automattic
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill providing WordPress block development guidance. All 212 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES: markdown backticks flagged as shell commands, documentation URLs flagged as external network calls, and benign filesystem scanning in list_blocks.mjs for locating block.json files. No malicious code execution, no credential access, no data exfiltration patterns detected.
valueStatement: WordPress block development requires understanding complex metadata, registration patterns, and asset management. This skill provides expert guidance for creating, configuring, and maintaining Gutenberg blocks using modern WordPress patterns and tooling.
limitations: Cannot execute npm scripts, wp-env, or run tests directly
Cannot modify files outside the skill instructions workflow
Limited to WP 6.9+ patterns; legacy patterns require manual research
Cannot access external WordPress installations or databases

useCases.title: Create WordPress plugins
useCases.description: Scaffold new block plugins with @wordpress/create-block for WordPress plugin directory submissions.

useCases.title: Add blocks to themes
useCases.description: Register custom blocks within themes using block.json metadata and PHP registration patterns.

useCases.title: Build dynamic blocks
useCases.description: Create server-rendered blocks with render.php for dynamic content like queries, menus, and auth states.

promptTemplates.title: Create new block
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new static block called 'my-custom-block' in a new plugin using @wordpress/create-block with TypeScript support.

promptTemplates.title: Fix invalid block
promptTemplates.prompt: My block is showing 'Invalid block' error after changing the save function. Help me add proper deprecation support.

promptTemplates.title: Dynamic rendering
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert my static block to use dynamic rendering with render.php that fetches data from WP_Query.

promptTemplates.title: Block migration
promptTemplates.prompt: Migrate my block to apiVersion 3 for WordPress 6.9 iframe editor compatibility. Check that all styles load correctly.

bestPractices: Always use apiVersion 3 in block.json for WordPress 6.9+ compatibility
Add deprecated versions when changing saved markup to prevent content breakage
antiPatterns: Changing block name after release (breaks existing content references)
Modifying saved markup without adding deprecated versions
Using deprecated 'meta' attribute source for new attributes

faq.question: What WordPress versions are supported?
faq.answer: Targets WordPress 6.9+ with PHP 7.2.24+. Older versions may work but lack apiVersion 3 support.

faq.question: Can this skill modify my plugin or theme files?
faq.answer: This skill provides guidance only. It cannot execute npm scripts or modify files directly.

faq.question: Does this work with build tools like @wordpress/scripts?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill references @wordpress/scripts commands but cannot run them directly.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill only reads block.json files locally. No data is transmitted externally.

faq.question: Why does my block show apiVersion warnings?
faq.answer: Update block.json to apiVersion: 3. This warning appears with SCRIPT_DEBUG enabled on WP 6.9+.

faq.question: How is this different from general coding AI?
faq.answer: Specialized WordPress block expertise with current best practices, deprecation handling, and WP-specific tooling knowledge.
